Overall: Very limited data is available on the effectiveness of current treatment technologies for the removal of terbufos from drinking water.  Terbufos and its degradates are reported to be unstable in water and degrade naturally in source waters via hydrolysis.  One study showed complete oxidation of terbufos with chlorine within one hour of contact time.  Another study showed 70 to >99 degradation with UV irradiation in the presence of TiO2 particles.
